answers.where: Mine Cards mining screen with a top action stage and lower idle board.
answers.action: The intended action is to watch Surface Stone mining and work toward the Copper Pickaxe, but the visual hierarchy does not make that obvious enough.
answers.response: Progress and geode reward can change, but the feedback moment is visually small and competes with status/chrome.
answers.reward: Stone, coins, XP, and upgrade cost are present, but the reward loop is still too text/panel driven.
answers.game_look: It resembles a debug/procedural idle layout more than a polished voxel idle RPG screen.

issues.text: The miner, pickaxe, and rock still do not read as one mining action; the miner is small/partial and the target contact is ambiguous.
The top stage is a large empty panel with a small action cluster and a disconnected status badge; it does not anchor the screen like the fake shot/reference direction.
The lower mechanics board still uses similar rectangular weights for active activity, future tabs, node cards, upgrade, and nav, so priority is hard to scan.
The screen still relies on procedural/runtime placeholder UI and placeholder icon treatment instead of a coherent generated/artist Mine Cards UI family.
Portrait exists, but it is a stacked version of the same dense panel language rather than a phone-first idle screen with a strong character/action hero.

not_covered.reason: No dedicated normal tick reward sequence reviewed in this pass.
Visible but still weak due placeholder badge/chip hierarchy.

problem: Lead-visible screenshots still do not read as a game: top action stage is disconnected/empty, character-target contact is weak, and landscape/portrait are not strong responsive compositions.
next: Freeze mechanic expansion. Produce a proper composition rescue: reference-backed stage layout, generated/artist UI/icon family, and native landscape+portrait screenshot proof before adding mechanics.
